pests.name: Leafhoppers
pests.impact: Transmits Phytoplasma disease (Cucurbitaceae Phytoplasma disease), causing small, thick, yellow-green leaves, short internodes, stunted plants, chrysanthemum-like appearance, and green flowers.
pests.solution: Control vectors (leafhoppers) using a recommended insecticide.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Initially appears as pale yellow spots on mature leaves, which enlarge as white powdery mycelium grows on the leaf surface. Severely affected leaves turn brown, shrivel, and drop.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development. Remove infected crop residues after harvest.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.impact: Angular yellow spots on the upper surface of leaves, limited by leaf veins. Leaves become chlorotic, eventually necrotic, and host plant cells die.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development. Remove infected plant parts after harvest.

diseases.name: Foot Rot / Basal Rot
diseases.impact: In early stages, plants show temporary wilting symptoms. Leaves turn yellow, wilt, and droop. Eventually, the plant dies. Vascular bundles at the base turn yellow or brown. White fungal networks are visible at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants with soil. Improve drainage. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development.

diseases.name: Gummy Stem Blight
diseases.impact: Lesions typically start in water-soaked areas on leaves and fruits. Early stages may have chlorotic holes, then turn light brown and irregular. Dark, cracked, sunken spots appear on fruits. Plants collapse at sunken, bark-cankered areas. Stem lesions often elongate like streaks, with gummy exudates from cracks. Dark brown to black pycnidia can be seen on fruits, stems, or leaves.
diseases.solution: Plow crop residues deep immediately after harvest to reduce fungal survival. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Sudden wilting of leaves, eventually the entire plant wilts. Leaves turn brown and dry. Vascular discoloration (browning). Permanent wilting. Spread by infected soil, water, crop residues, nematodes and insects, pruning, and weeds as host plants.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants with soil. Crop rotation with resistant varieties (okra, corn). Intercropping with Cruciferae family crops. Avoid transferring equipment from infected fields to uninfected fields.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus (LCV)
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves, short internodes, and distortion. Stunted plants and deformed fruits. Transmitted by Whitefly (Bemicia tabaci).

diseases.name: Papaya Ring Spot Virus (PRSV)
diseases.impact: Variegated spotted lesions on leaves. Leaf deformation. Fruit deformation and small fruit size. In severe infections, oily spots appear on the fruit surface. Transmitted by Aphids.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus (CMV)
diseases.impact: Green spots and leaf deformation. Green spots on fruits, turning bright yellow and decaying. Fruit deformation. Transmitted by seeds and sap.

diseases.name: Zucchini Yellow Mosaic Virus (ZYMV)
diseases.impact: Stunted plants, yellowing, chlorosis (occasionally), variegated deformed leaves (blisters). Fruit discoloration and deformation.

diseases.name: Cucurbitaceae Phytoplasma Disease / Small Leaf Disease
diseases.impact: Small, thick, and yellow-green leaves. Short internodes and stunted plants. Chrysanthemum-like appearance. Green flowers. Transmitted through infected seeds and leafhoppers.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants. Control vectors (leafhoppers) using a recommended insecticide.
